capabilities: parses source/include references with a depth limit and cycle detection (canonicalized keys)
records source_depth per file and the reference kind (ConfigSourceKind)
detects symlinks (is_symlink + symlink_target) and canonicalizes resolved paths
emits management hints (ConfigManagementHintKind, including SymlinkManaged) with a detection confidence
collects unsupported-source issues instead of failing

writeTargetSelection.rule: every gated scalar save writes the ConfigDiscovery-resolved active config; the pending diff shows that exact path so the user sees the real target before saving
writeTargetSelection.guiProof: b-03-saveonly-pending-page.png shows the diff header 'hyprland.conf' and the unified diff against /home/kyo/.config/hypr/hyprland.conf with the added line +binds:pass_mouse_when_bound = true
writeTargetSelection.unexpectedTargetPrevented: the save path never chooses an alternate/symlinked target implicitly; it writes the discovered active config only

duplicateSettingHandling.evidence: the dashboard 'Needs attention' card reads 'Some settings appear more than once in your config. Review them before applying changes.'
duplicateSettingHandling.diffContextEvidence: the save-only diff preview shows neighbouring duplicate context (misc:disable_hyprland_logo appears twice) drawn straight from the live config file
duplicateSettingHandling.note: duplicates are surfaced for review; the app does not blindly write duplicate values

luaHandling.finding: this machine runs a lua-configured Hyprland fork. Options set only through lua (not the parsed .conf) are invisible to the .conf parser. The app treats runtime-only lua values honestly: it never turns a runtime-only value into a fake config write.
luaHandling.saveOnlyImplication: save-only staged drafts write a real .conf line through the gate; they do not claim to reflect or override lua-side state. If a lua-set value diverges from the .conf, the diff shows the .conf change truthfully.
luaHandling.notGuessed: no lua parsing/rewriting is attempted; lua config remains out of scope until a trusted, safe lua read/write path exists

safeFallback: when a source cannot be safely modified (symlinked, generated, or lua-only), the app does not write it silently; scalar saves are confined to the discovered active config through the gate
